transcriptions.1: Hello there, I'm Kezia. I have been documenting in 360 for a good while and would like to share with you my process. Today, I'm happy to be sharing space with you at All Street NYC at 119 Hester St, Chinatown. And we will shortly begin our tutorial. Now let's go out!
transcriptions.2: The reason I brought us outside is because of the lighting. My gear is Insta360 X3 and while it performs nicely outdoors, especially on a sunny day, to be frank indoors or late daytime it is not the best. Well now we got ourselves in an unfortunate event where we're outside and it is not late yet, but the weather is horrible.
transcriptions.3: When we're thinking of traditional image, it is usually in rectangular form. Now as we capture in 360, what do you think will be the form? You may think that it is a circle, but it is actually still in a specific rectangular form called equirectangular projection.
transcriptions.4: Equirectangular form is mapped onto the inside of a virtual sphere, often rendered using Three.js on the web. Now we have the technology to look at it through a 2D screen-but should we look at it like a traditional photo, or does it ask for a different way of seeing?
transcriptions.5: If you're looking at the 360 image here, it is actually transforming a 2D rectangular image that contains information of the 360 environment into something that behaves like a 3D space that you can interact with. Isn't it quite mindboggling?
transcriptions.6: Not enough platforms are made yet to accommodate 360 images. Is website the best we can do to host the images? Or looking into a glass orb with the image in it is a better experience? How can we look or print a 360 image in physical life conveniently? And how can we do it digitally?
transcriptions.7: With 360 camera, the center point of view is of course dependent on where the camera is located at. With this fact in mind, you can basically be the center of the world if you choose to hold the camera while recording.
transcriptions.8: Earlier I asked if 360 image is still considered an 'image'. I think so, even though it is recording all the 360, the visual representation changes depending on where the center point of view is at. Instead of framing like in traditional camera, you change the center point of view in 360 camera.
transcriptions.9: I just came out from the underground. While 360 camera is capturing the environment around you, it does not capture the objects that are covered or blocked from the camera view. For example, the underground, the things behind the cars here, the interior inside the shop.
transcriptions.10: How do we look in 360 camera? I personally found my most appealing angle on 360 is when I just slightly hold the camera above me. The camera position right now that you're seeing is not very pleasing but this is the highest tripod that I have for now.
transcriptions.11: I found it interesting when subjects move from the front of the camera position towards its rear. You can see how the people were small and as it approaches the camera they don't only get larger, they get rounder! And they go back to being a small moving thing.
transcriptions.12: Be sure when taking picture with 360 to ensure all your fashion are on point. The camera really captures everything, including my broken shoes right now. It's my recent favorite shoes I got at Downtown Brooklyn but it literally broke after I broke into it.
transcriptions.13: A tip I'd give is to try shooting while in motion-riding something, for example. It starts to shift photography to be less about framing, and more about recording the world. As we carry cameras with us all the time, it begins to feel less like an intentional act, and more like a companion, or communication.
transcriptions.14: If you're standing in front of the 360 camera like me right now, the best view is typically around 2.5 to 3 feet. You're not too distorted and not too far away. But most importantly do not put the camera perpendicular to you as that is the stitching line.
transcriptions.15: You may wonder what is the stitching line? Just like the ATM that makes transaction between you and the bank, the stitching mechanism in a 360 connects the footage from the front and back lens. Be sure to avoid placing important subjects in this area as it may cut them off.
transcriptions.16: Honestly, today is not really a good day to take 360 image! The raindrop is blocking the lens and I suggest to put a protective lens over it. But I wonder if the raindrop itself can be a refractive 360 agent too..
transcriptions.17: Sidewalk gives us a lot of pedestrian movement, which can be interesting to capture in 360. The strength of 360 is that it sees left, right, top, front, and rear all at once. But right now, most of the activity is concentrated on our left.
transcriptions.18: The key to take interesting 360 image is similar to traditional camera. Find an interesting or striking subject and background. In this case, you have me, I'm interesting! But furthermore I added these lovely flowers and it goes so well with my floral dress.
transcriptions.19: Bold colors help to make the subject stand out as in 360 image. That's because you are showing audience way more items than in a traditional image. How can you make an order of interest within a 360 image? Or maybe that is just not the priority as it is "recording" not "framing".
transcriptions.20: Another view you shouldn't miss is building walls with interesting patterns or graffiti like this one. Subjects that stretch horizontally work especially well in 360 as you can capture the full span, and the image naturally loops back.
transcriptions.21: Now look at me, I'm coming from an angle higher from the camera. You can see how I look different as I move towards the camera. Try experimenting with the 360 camera by putting it at extremely low or high angle.
transcriptions.22: Now let's move on to taking 360 image in Chinatown, New York during the spring time. You may have noted the randomness of the area, and playful shop titles like E-SPA here or Choppy Cheese a few shops before, contrasted with the cherry blossom petals that got washed out by the rain underneath me. So random!
transcriptions.23: The kitschy environment sits side by side to resident buildings and sometimes also a large building like on my right here that dominates the camera view. For both 360 and traditional image, I really like finding visual cues that can fill in most of the image and becomes background inside a background.
transcriptions.24: I found that the Chinatown in New York City is somewhat similar to Mong Kok, Yau Ma Tei, and Jordan in Hong Kong. It brings me old memories! You will know what I mean if you go back to my 360 images collection in Jordan St, Hong Kong.
transcriptions.25: Today everything exists to end in a photograph, as Susan Sontag wrote. When everything is already photographed, is it still valid to say that we're photographing something? And if a single image attempts to capture everything at once, do you think snapping in 360 further distances us from the act of photographing?
transcriptions.26: Parallel to how much of our lives are recorded as data, photography also stores information, but with a specific context that is called axes. While traditional images have x and y, but we can argue that 360 virtually possess the z axis that encodes direction, letting us perceive a sense of space.
transcriptions.27: This perceived z axis allows depth to feel materialized as tangible information. It is not truly measured, but reconstructed by us as we look around. The space is not actually there, yet we experience it as if it is-so moving through it feels less like going somewhere, and more like circling.
transcriptions.28: All this walk around but have you noticed that we just did a circle around the block of Hester St, Forsyth St, Grand St, Eldridge St? Just like the 360 image, I want to make this tour experience a loop. A loop is interesting in an image context because typically you will need to reframe or crop out some parts of image that may prevent a loop from happening.
transcriptions.29: We're back at All Street! Tour's done and I hope you are all set to explore the world in 360! My name is Elizabeth Kezia Widjaja and I hope to see you again in another 360 universe.
